strengths: Clearly identifies three specific real-world objects and situates each in an authentic context
Explicit, well-explained links between each object and the chosen IA prompt
Sustained use of TOK terminology (e.g. justified true belief, faith) throughout the commentary
Most points supported by concrete evidence and citations (e.g. gem-testing protocols, historical examples)
Logical structure with clear headings and an effective conclusion synthesizing the TOK argument
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally lacks precision and nuance, particularly when dismissing religious justification
Some claims (e.g. seller reliability) are asserted without rigorous supporting evidence
Provenance details and image specificity are incomplete for the rosary and the Manifesto
Bibliographic citations are inconsistent and missing key publication details
Limited engagement with alternative perspectives and counterarguments
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition meets the descriptors for the top band: it clearly presents three objects in specific real-world contexts and offers strong, well-supported links to the IA prompt. The commentary demonstrates a sustained application of TOK concepts and evidence-based justification for each objectâs role in exploring knowledge, belief and opinion. Minor weaknessesâsuch as occasional over-generalizations, incomplete provenance details and inconsistent citation formattingâprevent an exceptional, fully nuanced treatment. These align with the lower end of the 9â10 band, making a final score of 9 appropriate.

annotations.comment: The description relies on general statements about Minas Geraisâs gem trade; deepening this with specific historical or economic details would strengthen the real-world context.
The claim that the store is âreliableâ is asserted without evidence; citing seller credentials or independent gem-testing reports would substantiate this justification.
The transition to Object 2 is clear and concise, maintaining the exhibitionâs logical flow and reinforcing the structure for the reader.
The rosary image appears generic; including provenance details or a personal photograph with metadata would strengthen its specificity.
Personal contextâreceiving the rosary from the studentâs motherâprovides an authentic connection to belief and enriches the objectâs real-world context.
The heading clearly labels Object 1 and situates it within a specific context, providing an immediate anchor for the reader into the exhibitionâs structure.
Effective use of the theory of justified true belief to classify the rose quartz pyramid as knowledge demonstrates a solid application of TOK terminology.
Reference to International Gem Society protocols adds credibility to the process of verifying the gemâs authenticity, reinforcing the knowledge claim.
The citation âClark, n.d.â lacks publication details (year, page), reducing the authority of the source. Include full bibliographic information for rigor.
The assertion that the region is âhistorically knownâ for gems would benefit from a specific historical reference or data to substantiate this claim.
The cultural and historical role of rosaries in Catholic practice is noted only briefly; deeper exploration would enhance the objectâs contextual relevance.
Object 3 is clearly introduced with title and context, maintaining consistency in object identification throughout the exhibition.
The image lacks edition details or provenance (purchase or archival history); adding such information would solidify the real-world context.
The citation for Woods 1997 is presented separately; integrating commentary on how it supports the analysis would clarify its relevance.
The conclusion effectively synthesizes why the Manifestoâs ideas qualify as opinions rather than knowledge, neatly closing the TOK argument.
Bibliography heading is present but the following entries lack a consistent citation style; adopt a single recognized format (APA, MLA, etc.) throughout.
The analysis effectively explains why faith, as represented by the rosary, meets the conditions of belief under justified true belief by highlighting its non-falsifiability.
By dismissing all theological justification, the analysis overlooks nuanced knowledge claims within religious epistemology; consider engaging alternative viewpoints.
Strong linkage of the Manifestoâs logical arguments to the concept of opinion under justified true belief demonstrates clear application of the TOK prompt.
The analysis would gain depth by addressing counterarguments or scholarly critiques of Marxâs thesis, enriching the TOK evaluation.
Distinctions between opinion and belief remain somewhat conflated; explicitly defining how opinion differs epistemologically would enhance clarity.
The entry for Marx & Engels (1848) lacks publisher or translator details; full bibliographic information is needed for academic rigor.
The rose quartz source omits author and access date; including these details would strengthen the referenceâs credibility.
Entries vary in punctuation and ordering (e.g., mixing URLs with embedded hyperlinks); standardizing formatting will improve professionalism.
âMarxists.org. 1848.â is too vague; clarify whether this refers to an online archive, its editors, or the edition consulted.
URLs listed without context make it unclear why each source was chosen; brief annotations of source relevance would guide the reader.
The Barlas 2023 entry omits publication medium (website, journal); specifying this information will complete the reference.
Repetitive listing of URLs in the bibliography could be streamlined by citing only the most authoritative sources and integrating others into the narrative.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
